task: Assess whether Datalox should present direct user value and commercial trajectory dataset value as one product or split them into separate products/offers.
step: decide whether to split user product and data product
summary: Clarified that Datalox should split the user-facing desktop agent product and the B2B trajectory data/eval product at the product and messaging level, while keeping one underlying Datalox MCP instrumentation/knowledge system. Mixing both promises in one user-facing product creates trust and positioning problems.
observations: The direct user product should be framed as a local-first desktop agent that gets better for the user through skills and notes.
The commercial product should be framed separately as consented, redacted, verified trajectory datasets/evals for AI companies.
The shared technical core can remain Datalox MCP: instrumentation, consent, event capture, verification, schema export, and local skill/note reuse.
title: Clarified that Datalox should split the user-facing desktop agent product and the B2B trajectory data/eval product at t…
signal: User questioned whether direct user value and B2B data value should be mixed in one product definition.
interpretation: The two loops should be separated as product surfaces/offers, not necessarily as separate codebases.
recommendedAction: Refactor docs to define Datalox MCP as the core platform, then separate Datalox Desktop/User Agent from Datalox Trajectory Data or Datalox Evals as distinct products.
